transcript.clean_text: the high ups for the venue for a day that will go down in history at the tour de france the day before stage 11 the tenth stage had been won by magnus court king of the mountains in denmark king of the big mountains the first time we got to them held on to the yellow jersey by just 11 seconds from leonard kempner who'd been in the breakaway jonas vingego would be at 39 seconds albertville venue for the winter olympics 30 years ago was the venue for the start of stage 11 a day that no cycling fan who was watching would ever forget finishing up the cold granola for the first time since 1986 the stage would head up the telegraph and the mighty galibi 151.7 kilometers from north to south finishing at the caldegranol venue for that victory by eduardo chossas in 1986. that will be the last day that bernard inor ever wore the yellow jersey what would be the fate of the yellow on this particular day mighty gradients all along the day over 4 000 meters of climbing and a dream breakaway to start up matcha fonda pool and watford are acting like it was boxing their soldered going away together fanart would extend his lead in the points classification taking 20 and 20 was the number of the riders up the road in the early breakaway it took 30 kilometers to form and would soon grab quite a gap going over the first la cidemon vernier climb then at the top of the telegraph it all kicked off yambo visma launching a huge attack fanart already up the road with laporte ready to contribute primos roglich and jonas finger girl going at kade pogaca like it was the 1950s copy bartali riders on their own almost epic stuff still with almost 60 k's to go matsuler really stuck up and stood to be counted he'd be there trying to defend things for uae emirates who already weakened by coronavirus meanwhile up the road up to the top of the tour's most used climb and the organizer ori dighange's was away behind though you couldn't take your eyes off it primoz roglic was at it again bulgarcha tried to defend from the front and you were left wondering if he'd be left to pay for the efforts thomas was reacting well naido quintana was there there abouts as was balder but quintana's teammate warren barghill would take the souvenir for the highest point of the tour 2640 meters they were crested with watforn art back in towards the end and helping to try and bring roglic back with vingagor a little further up the road was even lured into chasing things on the flat and once we got to the final climb roglich put in one big final dig cuss wouldn't last long and it would be up to rafael micah to try and sort things out for uae emirates nairo quintana launched an attack on that final climb the call du granot a narrow winding road with gradients well above 10 percent in places oman balder would then set off from the pogacha bunch still mike are doing his job at this point and then vinegar just over five kilometers when he attacked and the man who'd been the closest to pogacha last year was ready to threaten him this time out quintana would reach the front of the race winger would go straight past baldi michael dunn and pogaccha showing weakness for the first time in his career caroline thomas going beyond pogacad vingagor getting past quintana and moving into the yellow virtual jersey even yates and goldu were getting the better of a pogaccha in crisis and jonas finger rode on and finished the job an appointment with history jumbo visma having the courage to take it on 60 kilometers from the finish on some of the toughest roads that the tour de france had ever presented its contestants a first tour de france stage win for him the 39 seconds passed he was in the yellow jersey naido quintana moved up in the general classification as did homomaldi now on the podium garrett thomas into fourth quintana down in fifth tadei pogachar losing almost three minutes on the road and after a stage for the ages he'd now be two minutes and 22 seconds behind in third position jonas finger at 25 years of age will be the new leader victory by almost a minute from quintana a minute and 10 from baldi with thomas and gordoo in the top five yates finishing ahead of pogacad who really slipped and slid down into seventh on the day exhausted but still gentlemanly in his congratulations great sportsmanship shown at a new yellow jersey a perfect day for cycling one that will rarely be bettered entertainment to the end vingagor the new leader a two-minute 16 lead over human body bulgacha still on the podium but just four seconds ahead of thomas quintana in the top five it's still all to play for and address comes the day after 165.1 kilometers it was all change today three all category climbs to come and climbing up the galilee again from the start this time from priyansa a special day on day 11 one we will never forget all chance for all change again maybe on stage 12. it's bastille day two time for the first french win you'll better find out by watching on gcn plus
